purpose: High-level overview of Synapse's file-based data architecture: no database, JSON files watched by the server and broadcast via SSE, strict file ownership per writer, and derived (not stored) statistics. Explains the static-plan vs dynamic-lifecycle vs event-log split, the mergeState client-side merge, and the file-watching mechanisms (fs.watchFile polling for init/logs, fs.watch for progress).
gotchas: Statistics are derived at render time -- never stored as counters; modifying counters introduces drift
fs.watch on progress/ uses OS events; fs.watchFile on init/logs polls at 100ms intervals
Reconciliation runs every 5s as a safety net for missed OS events on network filesystems
patterns: Derived state from authoritative file reads
conventions: Default empty states defined in src/server/utils/constants.js (DEFAULT_INITIALIZATION, DEFAULT_LOGS)
